info.title: Warmbly API
info.description: The Warmbly API lets you drive mailboxes, campaigns, contacts, the unibox, CRM, and more programmatically. Authenticate with an API key as a Bearer token. All paths are relative to the versioned base URL.

tags.name: auth
tags.description: Authentication: login, registration, password reset, 2FA, and sessions.

tags.name: mailboxes
tags.description: Connected sending mailboxes and their warmup lifecycle.

tags.name: campaigns
tags.description: Cold outreach campaigns, steps, and A/B variants.

tags.name: unibox
tags.description: Unified inbox: threads, replies, and labels.

tags.name: api-keys
tags.description: API key management and usage logs.

tags.name: account-org
tags.description: Account, organization, and plan reference data.

tags.name: deliverability-ops
tags.description: Deliverability event ingest, suppression, and seed placement.

post.description: Step 1 of email login. Verifies the email/password and Turnstile token, then emails a one-time confirmation code. Returns an opaque session handle to pass to /auth/login/confirm.

200.description: Confirmation code sent; returns the session handle.

post.description: Step 2 of email login. Exchanges the session handle plus the emailed code for a token pair. If 2FA is enabled, returns a 2FA challenge (pending_token) instead of a session.

200.description: Login result: either a full token pair, or a 2FA challenge.

401.description: Wrong or expired code, or invalid session.

post.description: Step 1 of registration. Validates email/password and Turnstile, then emails a confirmation code. Returns an opaque session handle for /auth/register/confirm.

200.description: Confirmation code sent; returns the session handle.

400.description: Invalid request body or weak password.

401.description: Email already in use or not allowed.

post.description: Step 2 of registration. Exchanges the session handle plus the emailed code to finalize the account. Returns 204 on success.

401.description: Wrong or expired code, or invalid session.

post.description: Exchanges a valid refresh token for a new token pair (rotating refresh).

401.description: Refresh token invalid, expired, or revoked.

post.description: Sends a password-reset code to the email if an account exists. Always returns 200 to avoid account enumeration.

200.description: Reset email sent if the account exists.

post.description: Exchanges the reset session handle plus a new password to set a new password.

400.description: Invalid request body or weak password.

post.description: Exchanges the single-use pending_token from /auth/login/confirm plus a TOTP or recovery code for a real token pair.

401.description: Pending token invalid/expired, or code wrong.

post.description: Starts a discoverable/usernameless passkey login. Returns the WebAuthn assertion options plus an opaque session handle to pass to /auth/passkey/login/finish.

200.description: WebAuthn assertion options and the login session handle.

post.description: Submits the WebAuthn assertion together with the login session handle. On success returns a full token pair.
Revokes the session bound to the bearer access token. Requires a user session token (not an API key).
Revokes every active session for the authenticated user. Requires a user session token (not an API key).

get.description: Returns the current user profile, including per-user folders, tags, and categories. Requires a user session token (not an API key).

patch.description: Updates basic profile fields for the current user. Requires a user session token (not an API key).

post.description: Changes the signed-in user's password (current + new). Requires a user session token (not an API key).

400.description: Invalid request body or weak new password.

401.description: Current password wrong or session invalid.

get.description: Lists the authenticated user's active sessions, with the caller's current session flagged. Requires a user session token (not an API key). This endpoint returns a plain array, not a paginated wrapper.

delete.description: Ends every active session except the current one. Requires a user session token (not an API key).
Ends one of the user's sessions by id. Requires a user session token (not an API key).

get.description: Reports whether the authenticated user has 2FA enabled. Requires a user session token (not an API key).

post.description: Generates a fresh TOTP secret and otpauth provisioning URI (shown once). Requires a user session token (not an API key).

200.description: The TOTP secret and otpauth URI.

post.description: Verifies a TOTP code, enables 2FA, and returns one-time recovery codes (shown once). Requires a user session token (not an API key).

delete.description: Turns off 2FA for the user. Requires a current TOTP or recovery code in the body. Requires a user session token (not an API key).

get.description: Lists the authenticated user's registered passkeys. Requires a user session token (not an API key). Returns a plain array, not a paginated wrapper.

patch.description: Renames a registered passkey by id. Requires a user session token (not an API key).

delete.description: Removes a registered passkey by id. Requires a user session token (not an API key).

get.description: Returns the organization's connected mailboxes, newest first, with cursor pagination.

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: Free-text search over mailbox address and name.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque pagination token from a previous pagination.next_cursor.

get.description: Returns a single mailbox by id.

403.description: Insufficient scope, permission, or mailbox not allowed for this key.

patch.description: Updates mailbox settings. All fields are optional; only present fields are applied.

403.description: Insufficient scope, permission, or mailbox not allowed for this key.

delete.description: Disconnects and deletes a mailbox. It is removed from all warmup pools.

403.description: Insufficient scope, permission, or mailbox not allowed for this key.

patch.description: Sets or clears the custom open/click tracking domain for a mailbox. Send an empty domain to clear it and fall back to the shared default.

parameters.name: domain
parameters.description: The custom tracking subdomain (for example t.acme.com). Empty clears it.

403.description: Insufficient scope, permission, or mailbox not allowed for this key.

post.description: Enables warmup for a mailbox. When resuming from a paused state it preserves ramp progress.

403.description: Insufficient scope, permission, or mailbox not allowed for this key.

post.description: Pauses warmup without losing ramp progress. A later start continues from the same daily volume.

200.description: The updated mailbox. A paused mailbox has a non-null warmup_paused_at.

403.description: Insufficient scope, permission, or mailbox not allowed for this key.

post.description: Resumes a paused warmup, shifting the ramp anchor forward so progress continues where it left off.

200.description: The updated mailbox, with warmup_paused_at cleared.

403.description: Insufficient scope, permission, or mailbox not allowed for this key.

post.description: Disables warmup entirely and clears ramp progress. A later start begins a fresh ramp.

200.description: The updated mailbox, with warmup disabled.

403.description: Insufficient scope, permission, or mailbox not allowed for this key.

get.description: Validates SPF, DKIM, and DMARC for the mailbox's sending domain on demand.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The mailbox id. The domain is derived from the mailbox address.

403.description: Insufficient scope, permission, or mailbox not allowed for this key.

post.description: Verifies a single email address on demand (syntax, MX, SMTP RCPT probe, catch-all detection). The address may be supplied in the JSON body or as the email query param; the body takes precedence.

parameters.name: email
parameters.description: The address to verify. Used when not supplied in the body.

get.description: Returns whether a mailbox is blocked from the shared warmup pool, why, and whether the owner can appeal.

403.description: Insufficient scope, permission, or mailbox not allowed for this key.

post.description: Lets the mailbox owner appeal a warmup ban with a reason.

403.description: Insufficient scope, permission, or mailbox not allowed for this key.

post.description: Sends a one-off email from a specific mailbox, scheduled and dispatched through the mailbox's assigned worker. Requires an active organization.

400.description: Invalid id, request body, or no active organization.

403.description: Insufficient scope, permission, or mailbox not allowed for this key.

get.description: Search and page through the organization's campaigns. Scope READ_CAMPAIGNS, org permission view_campaigns.

parameters.name: folder
parameters.description: Restrict to a single folder id.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque pagination cursor from the previous page.

post.description: Create a campaign. Only name is required. Scope WRITE_CAMPAIGNS, org permission manage_campaigns.

get.description: Fetch a single campaign by id. Scope READ_CAMPAIGNS, org permission view_campaigns.

patch.description: Patch any subset of campaign fields. Omitted fields are unchanged. Scope WRITE_CAMPAIGNS, org permission manage_campaigns.

delete.description: Permanently delete a campaign. Scope WRITE_CAMPAIGNS, org permission manage_campaigns.

get.description: Return the campaign's advanced outreach overrides. Scope READ_CAMPAIGNS, org permission view_campaigns.

patch.description: Replace the campaign's advanced overrides. Scope WRITE_CAMPAIGNS, org permission manage_settings.

get.description: List the campaign's A/B variants. Scope READ_CAMPAIGNS, org permission view_campaigns.

post.description: Add a variant to the campaign (or one step via step_id). Scope WRITE_CAMPAIGNS, org permission manage_settings.

patch.description: Patch a variant. Omitted fields are unchanged. Scope WRITE_CAMPAIGNS, org permission manage_settings.

delete.description: Remove a variant. Scope WRITE_CAMPAIGNS, org permission manage_settings.

get.description: Return per-variant engagement stats and the computed winner. Scope READ_ANALYTICS, org permission view_analytics.
List the campaign's attachments, each with a short-lived presigned download url. Scope READ_CAMPAIGNS, org permission view_campaigns.

post.description: Upload a file (max 15 MB) to attach to the campaign or one step. Multipart form data. Scope WRITE_CAMPAIGNS, org permission manage_campaigns.

file.description: The file to upload (max 15 MB). Executable and script types are rejected.

step_id.description: Scope the attachment to one sequence step.

400.description: Validation error or rejected file type.

delete.description: Delete a campaign attachment and its stored object. Scope WRITE_CAMPAIGNS, org permission manage_campaigns.

post.description: Run the campaign's preflight validation checks and return a scored report. No mail is sent. Scope SEND_CAMPAIGNS, org permission send_campaigns.
Send a one-off preview of a sequence step to a recipient through a chosen mailbox. Scope SEND_CAMPAIGNS, org permission send_campaigns.
Activate the campaign so it begins sending real mail. Scope SEND_CAMPAIGNS, org permission send_campaigns.

400.description: Campaign not in a startable state.

post.description: Pause an active campaign. Scope SEND_CAMPAIGNS, org permission send_campaigns.

400.description: Campaign not in a stoppable state.

get.description: Page through the campaign's activity log. Scope READ_CAMPAIGNS, org permission view_campaigns.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque pagination cursor from the previous page.

get.description: Return the campaign's explicit sender pool. Scope READ_CAMPAIGNS, org permission view_campaigns.

put.description: Atomically replace the campaign's explicit sender pool. Scope WRITE_CAMPAIGNS, org permission manage_campaigns.

post.description: Resolve the campaign-scoped tracking domain's CNAME and flip tracking_domain_verified on success. Scope WRITE_CAMPAIGNS, org permission manage_campaigns.

400.description: Verification failed or no domain configured.

get.description: Return the campaign's sequence steps in order. Scope READ_CAMPAIGNS, org permission view_campaigns.

200.description: The sequence steps (bare array, no envelope).

post.description: Append a new empty sequence step created with defaults, then edited with PATCH. No request body. Scope WRITE_CAMPAIGNS, org permission manage_campaigns.

patch.description: Patch a sequence step: copy, spacing, node kind, branching tree, or action config. Omitted fields are unchanged. Scope WRITE_CAMPAIGNS, org permission manage_campaigns.

delete.description: Delete a sequence step. Scope WRITE_CAMPAIGNS, org permission manage_campaigns.

post.description: Render subject and body templates against a sample (or supplied) contact and report parse errors plus unresolved tokens. No side effects. Scope READ_CAMPAIGNS, org permission view_campaigns.

post.summary: Generate copy with the writing assistant
post.description: Generate outreach copy with the AI writing assistant. Gated to paid and free-trial orgs; consumes one AI credit (refunded on provider failure). Scope WRITE_CAMPAIGNS, org permission manage_campaigns.
Faceted, org-scoped contact search. Filters live in the body; pagination is via query params. Scope `READ_CONTACTS`.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque pagination cursor from the previous page's pagination.next_cursor.

parameters.name: category
parameters.description: Convenience filter for a single category ID.

requestBody.description: All filters optional; an empty body matches every contact in the organization.

403.description: API key lacks READ_CONTACTS or caller lacks view_contacts.

post.description: Creates one or more contacts. The body is a JSON array, so a single create is an array of length one. Scope `WRITE_CONTACTS`.

200.description: The created contacts as a bare array.

400.description: Empty array or too many contacts.

403.description: API key lacks WRITE_CONTACTS or caller lacks manage_contacts.

patch.description: Applies one set of edits across up to 1000 contacts: add/remove campaigns and categories, custom-field operations, and subscription. Scope `BULK_CONTACTS`.

200.description: The updated contacts as a bare array.

400.description: No contacts provided or more than 1000.

403.description: API key lacks BULK_CONTACTS or caller lacks manage_contacts.

delete.description: Deletes up to 1000 contacts by ID. Scope `BULK_CONTACTS`.

requestBody.description: A JSON array of contact ID strings (1 to 1000).

400.description: Empty array or more than 1000 IDs.

403.description: API key lacks BULK_CONTACTS or caller lacks manage_contacts.

post.description: Exports contacts to CSV, XLSX, or JSON. The response is the file itself, not JSON. Capped at 50,000 rows. Scope `READ_CONTACTS`.

200.description: The export file as an attachment.

403.description: API key lacks READ_CONTACTS or caller lacks view_contacts.

post.description: Uploads a CSV or XLSX file and returns detected columns plus a sample so the client can build a column mapping. Uploads capped at 50 MB. Scope `WRITE_CONTACTS`.

200.description: Detected columns, sample rows, and a suggested mapping.

400.description: Missing file, unsupported format, or over the size cap.

403.description: API key lacks WRITE_CONTACTS or caller lacks manage_contacts.

post.description: Re-uploads the file with a mapping and dedup options, applies it, and returns per-row results. Imports capped at 50,000 rows. Scope `BULK_CONTACTS`.

400.description: Missing file/options, invalid mapping or dedup, or over a cap.

403.description: API key lacks BULK_CONTACTS or caller lacks manage_contacts.

get.summary: Look up a contact by email
get.description: Resolves a sender address to a contact. Returns 200 with {"contact": null} when nothing matches. A display-name wrapped address (`Name <addr@example.com>`) is accepted and unwrapped. Scope `READ_CONTACTS`.

403.description: API key lacks READ_CONTACTS or caller lacks view_contacts.

get.description: Returns the hydrated contact 360 payload: the contact plus an engagement summary and, when present, suppression state. Scope `READ_CONTACTS`.

403.description: API key lacks READ_CONTACTS or caller lacks view_contacts.

patch.description: Partially updates a single contact; only the fields present change. Category lists can be set wholesale or adjusted with diff-style add/remove. Scope `WRITE_CONTACTS`.

403.description: API key lacks WRITE_CONTACTS or caller lacks manage_contacts.

delete.description: Deletes a single contact. Scope `WRITE_CONTACTS`.

403.description: API key lacks WRITE_CONTACTS or caller lacks manage_contacts.

get.summary: List emails sent to a contact
get.description: One row per email sent (or attempted) to the contact, newest first. Keyset paginated on (created_at, task_id); pass both before_at and before_id together. Scope `READ_CONTACTS`.

parameters.name: before_at
parameters.description: created_at of the last row from the previous page (RFC 3339 nano).

parameters.name: before_id
parameters.description: task_id of the last row from the previous page.

403.description: API key lacks READ_CONTACTS or caller lacks view_contacts.

get.description: Merged activity feed: sends, opens, clicks, replies, bounces, deliverability/suppression events, notes, and meeting bookings. Requires a selected organization. Paginate via the `before` timestamp. Scope `READ_CONTACTS`.

parameters.name: before
parameters.description: The `at` timestamp of the oldest event from the previous page (RFC 3339 nano).

200.description: Timeline events with a has_more flag.

400.description: Invalid contact ID, or no organization selected.

403.description: API key lacks READ_CONTACTS or caller lacks view_contacts.

get.description: Structured CRM activity log for a contact. Requires a selected organization. Scope `READ_CONTACTS`.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque cursor from the previous page's pagination.next_cursor.

400.description: Invalid contact ID, cursor, or no organization selected.

403.description: API key lacks READ_CONTACTS or caller lacks view_contacts.

get.description: CRM notes attached to a contact, newest first. Requires a selected organization. Scope `READ_CONTACTS`.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque cursor from the previous page's pagination.next_cursor.

400.description: Invalid contact ID, cursor, or no organization selected.

403.description: API key lacks READ_CONTACTS or caller lacks view_contacts.

post.description: Adds a note to a contact. Requires a selected organization. Scope `WRITE_CONTACTS`.

400.description: Invalid contact ID, missing/too-long content, or no organization selected.

403.description: API key lacks WRITE_CONTACTS or caller lacks manage_contacts.

patch.description: Edits a note's content. Requires a selected organization. Scope `WRITE_CONTACTS`.

400.description: Invalid IDs, body, or no organization selected.

403.description: API key lacks WRITE_CONTACTS or caller lacks manage_contacts.

delete.description: Deletes a note. Requires a selected organization. Scope `WRITE_CONTACTS`.

400.description: Invalid IDs or no organization selected.

403.description: API key lacks WRITE_CONTACTS or caller lacks manage_contacts.

get.description: CRM deals associated with a contact, returned as a bare JSON array. Scope `READ_CRM`.

403.description: API key lacks READ_CRM or caller lacks view_contacts.

get.description: Org-wide inbox list, collapsed to one row per thread (newest message), with filtering and cursor pagination. Excludes snoozed threads unless `snoozed=true`.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque pagination cursor from a previous response.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Page size, clamped to the server min/max.

parameters.name: unseen
parameters.description: `true` returns only threads with unread messages.

parameters.name: awaiting_reply
parameters.description: `true` returns only threads whose latest message was sent by you.

parameters.name: snoozed
parameters.description: `true` returns only snoozed threads. Omit to exclude snoozed threads.

parameters.name: email_id
parameters.description: Restrict to a single mailbox by UUID.

parameters.name: email_ids
parameters.description: Comma-separated mailbox UUIDs. A thread matches if it landed in any of them.

parameters.name: category_ids
parameters.description: Comma-separated conversation-label UUIDs. A thread matches if it carries any of them.

400.description: Invalid cursor, limit, or no organization selected.

403.description: Missing scope/permission, or organization lacks unified-inbox access.

get.description: Org-wide unread message count, optionally scoped to one mailbox.

parameters.name: email_id
parameters.description: Optional mailbox UUID to count unread for a single mailbox.

403.description: Missing scope/permission, or organization lacks unified-inbox access.

get.description: Rolls up scope-rail and metric-strip counts (unread, today, week, snoozed, awaiting-reply, pending-scheduled) plus per-mailbox, per-tag, and per-conversation-label breakdowns.

403.description: Missing scope/permission, or organization lacks unified-inbox access.

get.description: Every message in a single conversation, with cursor pagination. With no `email_id` the thread is read across every mailbox in the organization.

parameters.name: thread_id
parameters.description: The thread to read. Also accepted as `id`.

parameters.name: email_id
parameters.description: Optional mailbox UUID to scope the thread to one mailbox. Also accepted as `email`.

400.description: Missing `thread_id`, invalid cursor/limit, or no organization selected.

403.description: Missing scope/permission, or organization lacks unified-inbox access.

get.description: Conversation labels (your categories) attached to a thread.

parameters.name: thread_id
parameters.description: The thread to read labels for. Also accepted as `id`.

200.description: Label set wrapped in a `data` array.

403.description: Missing scope/permission, or organization lacks unified-inbox access.

put.description: Replaces the full conversation-label set on a thread. `category_ids` is the desired set, so the call is idempotent and retries are naturally safe.

200.description: Resulting label set wrapped in a `data` array.

403.description: Missing scope/permission, or organization lacks unified-inbox access.

patch.description: Marks a batch of messages as read or unread, org-wide. Up to 500 ids per call.

400.description: Invalid body, more than 500 ids, or no organization selected.

403.description: Missing scope/permission, or organization lacks unified-inbox access.

post.description: Sends or schedules a reply from one of your mailboxes, routed through the per-mailbox scheduler according to `send_mode`. Requires an active organization.

400.description: Invalid body, invalid mailbox UUID, missing future `scheduled_at`, or no organization selected.

403.description: Missing scope/permission, or organization lacks unified-inbox access.

get.description: Your active thread snoozes.

200.description: Active snoozes wrapped in a `data` array.

403.description: Missing scope/permission, or organization lacks unified-inbox access.

post.description: Hides a thread from your inbox until `snoozed_until` passes. Upsert semantics: a second call on the same thread updates the time in place.

400.description: Invalid body or no organization selected.

403.description: Missing scope/permission, or organization lacks unified-inbox access.

delete.description: Un-snoozes a thread immediately. Idempotent: deleting a snooze that does not exist still succeeds with 204.

204.description: Snooze removed (or already absent). Empty body.

403.description: Missing scope/permission, or organization lacks unified-inbox access.

get.description: Outbound emails you have queued but not yet sent. Pass `thread_id` to scope to a single conversation; the response shape is identical either way.

parameters.name: thread_id
parameters.description: Restrict to scheduled sends queued into one thread.

200.description: Queued message previews wrapped in a `data` array.

403.description: Missing scope/permission, or organization lacks unified-inbox access.

delete.description: Cancels a pending scheduled send before it fires. The queued task is marked cancelled and short-circuits to a no-op when its run time arrives.

parameters.name: task_id
parameters.description: UUID of the scheduled task to cancel.

400.description: Invalid task id or no organization selected.

403.description: Missing scope/permission, or organization lacks unified-inbox access.

get.description: A single message by its UUID, including the full envelope and body.

400.description: Invalid message id or no organization selected.

403.description: Missing scope/permission, or organization lacks unified-inbox access.

get.description: Return every pipeline in the organization, each with its ordered stages. Returns a bare array, not a list envelope.

200.description: Array of pipelines (each with its stages).

post.description: Create a pipeline, optionally seeding it with an ordered set of stages.

get.description: Fetch a single pipeline with its stages.

patch.description: Rename a pipeline. Only the name can be changed here; stages have their own endpoints.

400.description: Invalid request body or path parameter.

delete.description: Delete a pipeline and its stages.

post.description: Append a stage to a pipeline.

400.description: Invalid request body or path parameter.

patch.description: Rename or recolor a stage.

400.description: Invalid request body or path parameter.

delete.description: Remove a stage from a pipeline.

get.description: List deals with optional pipeline, stage, and status filters, keyset-paginated.

parameters.name: pipeline_id
parameters.description: Restrict to deals in this pipeline.

parameters.name: stage_id
parameters.description: Restrict to deals in this stage.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque keyset cursor from a previous page's pagination.next_cursor.

400.description: Invalid cursor, limit, or filter value.

post.description: Create a deal in a pipeline stage, optionally linked to a contact and attributed to a campaign and source mailbox. New deals default to status open.
Faceted, offset-paginated deal search. Every filter is optional; an empty body matches every deal in the organization. Filters go in the JSON body; limit and offset are query params.

200.description: Offset-paginated deal results with an exact total.

400.description: Invalid limit, offset, or filter body.

post.description: Aggregate counts and value sums over the same filter body as deal search, including per-stage totals. All facets are optional.

get.description: Fetch a single deal. Joined contact, stage, and campaign_name are only populated by the list and search queries, not by this single-row read.

patch.description: Update a deal. Moving it to a different stage_id records a stage-change activity, and setting status to won or lost stamps the corresponding close timestamp.

400.description: Invalid request body or path parameter.

delete.description: Delete a deal.

get.description: List the organization's CRM task types. A default set is seeded the first time an org lists its types. Returns a data array with no pagination envelope.

post.description: Create a CRM task type.

patch.description: Rename, recolor, or reorder a task type.

400.description: Invalid request body or path parameter.

delete.description: Delete a task type. Tasks reference their type by name, so existing tasks keep their label and fall back to a neutral color rather than being orphaned.

get.description: List CRM tasks with optional contact, deal, assignee, and status filters, keyset-paginated.

parameters.name: contact_id
parameters.description: Restrict to tasks linked to this contact.

parameters.name: deal_id
parameters.description: Restrict to tasks linked to this deal.

parameters.name: assigned_to
parameters.description: Restrict to tasks assigned to this user.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque keyset cursor from a previous page's pagination.next_cursor.

400.description: Invalid cursor, limit, or filter value.

post.description: Create a CRM task, optionally linked to a contact and deal and assigned to a user or team. created_by is set to the authenticated user.
Faceted, offset-paginated task search. Every filter is optional; an empty body matches every task in the organization. Filters go in the JSON body; limit and offset are query params.

200.description: Offset-paginated task results with an exact total.

400.description: Invalid limit, offset, or filter body.

post.description: Aggregate counts over the same filter body as task search (by status, overdue, high priority). All facets are optional.

get.description: Fetch a single CRM task.

patch.description: Update a CRM task. Setting status to completed stamps the completion timestamp.

400.description: Invalid request body or path parameter.

delete.description: Delete a CRM task.

get.description: Returns the organization's API keys, newest first. The plaintext secret is never included.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque pagination token from the previous page's pagination.next_cursor. Omit for the first page.

400.description: Invalid cursor or limit, or no organization selected.

403.description: Missing API_KEYS scope or manage_api_keys org permission.

post.description: Creates a new key and returns the plaintext secret exactly once. Unknown permission bits are rejected.

201.description: The created key, including the one-time secret.

400.description: Invalid body, no organization selected, or permission bitmask contains unknown bits.

403.description: Missing API_KEYS scope or manage_api_keys org permission.

get.description: Returns the catalog of permission bits plus the read_only and full_access presets.

403.description: Missing API_KEYS scope or manage_api_keys org permission.

get.description: Org-level usage strip: key counts by status plus a 24-hour request, error, and latency rollup.

403.description: Missing API_KEYS scope or manage_api_keys org permission.

get.description: Time-bucketed request series plus a per-endpoint breakdown for the whole organization. For this org-wide form api_key_id is the all-zero UUID.

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: Start of the window (RFC3339). Defaults to 24 hours before to.

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: End of the window (RFC3339). Defaults to now.

403.description: Missing API_KEYS scope or manage_api_keys org permission.

get.description: Returns a single key by id. The secret is never included.

403.description: Missing API_KEYS scope or manage_api_keys org permission.

404.description: Invalid UUID or key not found in this organization.

patch.description: Updates the mutable fields of a key. Every field is optional; only the fields you send are changed. The secret cannot be rotated here.

400.description: Invalid body, no organization selected, or permission bitmask contains unknown bits.

403.description: Missing API_KEYS scope or manage_api_keys org permission.

404.description: Invalid UUID or key not found in this organization.

delete.description: Revokes a key immediately. The key stops authenticating right away; this is not reversible.

parameters.name: reason
parameters.description: Optional revocation note stored on the key. Defaults to "Revoked by user".

403.description: Missing API_KEYS scope or manage_api_keys org permission.

404.description: Invalid UUID or key not found in this organization.

get.description: Time-bucketed request series plus a per-endpoint breakdown for a single key. Pass the literal id value `all` for the org-wide aggregate.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The API key id, or the literal `all` for the org-wide aggregate.

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: Start of the window (RFC3339). Defaults to 24 hours before to.

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: End of the window (RFC3339). Defaults to now.

403.description: Missing API_KEYS scope or manage_api_keys org permission.

404.description: Invalid UUID (when not `all`) or key not found in this organization.

get.description: Returns the recent raw request entries for a single key, newest first.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque pagination token from the previous page's pagination.next_cursor.

200.description: A page of usage log entries.

400.description: Invalid cursor, or no organization selected.

403.description: Missing API_KEYS scope or manage_api_keys org permission.

404.description: Invalid UUID or key not found in this organization.

get.description: Returns every webhook endpoint configured for the caller's organization, plus the canonical `event_types` vocabulary for building a picker. Secrets are never returned. This endpoint does NOT use the `data` + `pagination` cursor envelope. Requires the `WEBHOOKS` scope and the `manage_settings` org permission.

200.description: The organization's webhook endpoints and the full event vocabulary.

post.description: Creates a new event subscription. The `url` must be HTTPS and resolve to a publicly routable host (loopback, private, and link-local targets are rejected unless the server runs with unsafe webhook URLs enabled for local or self-hosted development). The response is the only time the signing `secret` (prefixed `whsec_`) is returned, so capture it immediately. Requires the `WEBHOOKS` scope and the `manage_settings` org permission.

201.description: The created endpoint, including the one-time `secret`.

400.description: Invalid payload, non-HTTPS or non-routable url, or unknown event type.

patch.description: Replaces the endpoint's url, description, event filter, and enabled state with the values sent (send the complete desired state; `event_types` is overwritten, not merged). The signing secret is not changed here; use the rotate-secret endpoint. Requires the `WEBHOOKS` scope and the `manage_settings` org permission.

400.description: Invalid payload, invalid endpoint id, non-HTTPS or non-routable url, or unknown event type.

404.description: Endpoint not found or not owned by your organization.

delete.description: Deletes a subscription and cascades to its delivery history. Requires the `WEBHOOKS` scope and the `manage_settings` org permission.

404.description: Endpoint not found or not owned by your organization.

post.description: Issues a new HMAC signing secret and returns it once. In-flight deliveries already signed continue to verify against the old secret until they settle; new deliveries use the new secret. Update your `X-Warmbly-Signature` verifier promptly. Requires the `WEBHOOKS` scope and the `manage_settings` org permission.

200.description: The new signing secret. Returned only once.

404.description: Endpoint not found or not owned by your organization.

get.description: Returns recent delivery attempts for an endpoint, newest first. Each row updates in place across retries, so an event that retried several times appears as one record whose `attempt_count` and `status` reflect the latest state. This endpoint does NOT use the `data` + `pagination` cursor envelope; it returns a `deliveries` array bounded by `limit`. Requires the `WEBHOOKS` scope and the `manage_settings` org permission.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The endpoint id whose deliveries to list.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Max rows to return. Between 1 and 200. Defaults to 50. Out-of-range values return 400.

200.description: Recent delivery attempts for the endpoint.

400.description: Invalid endpoint id or a limit outside 1 to 200.

404.description: Endpoint not found or not owned by your organization.

get.description: Org-wide dashboard overview: aggregate stats, recent activity, top campaigns, account health, and a daily trend series.

parameters.name: period
parameters.description: One of 7d, 30d, 90d. Any other value falls back to 7d.

get.description: Deliverability posture over a window: bounce/complaint/open/click/reply counts and rates, suppression and dead-letter pressure, reply-intent breakdown, seed inbox-placement, health band, daily timeseries, and per-mailbox/per-campaign breakdowns.

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: Window start as an RFC 3339 timestamp. Defaults to 7 days ago (UTC).

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: Window end as an RFC 3339 timestamp. Defaults to now (UTC).

get.description: Warmup send and reply statistics over a date range, with a summary and per-day series. Optionally scoped to a single mailbox.

parameters.name: email_id
parameters.description: Limit to one email account. Invalid UUIDs are ignored.

get.description: Side-by-side performance for up to 10 campaigns over a date range. Every requested campaign must belong to the caller.

parameters.name: ids
parameters.description: Comma-separated campaign UUIDs. Invalid entries are dropped; capped at 10. At least one valid id is required.

404.description: A requested campaign was not found.

get.description: A single campaign's performance summary plus per-step stats. The campaign must belong to the caller.
Per-day send, open, click, and reply counts for one campaign over a date range. The campaign must belong to the caller.

200.description: Per-day series under a data envelope.

get.description: Per-hour stats for one campaign on a single day. The campaign must belong to the caller.

parameters.name: date
parameters.description: Day to report (YYYY-MM-DD). Defaults to today.

200.description: Per-hour series under a data envelope with the resolved date echoed back.

get.description: Health and usage status of every email account the caller owns. Returned under a data envelope (no cursor; all accounts included). Accounts that fail to build are skipped.

Detailed status for one email account: combined health score (folding in warmup-pool reputation), active errors, today's usage, warmup status, and warmup-pool health. The account must belong to the caller.
Account, campaign, contact, and API usage counters for the caller.

parameters.name: period
parameters.description: One of day, week, month. Any other value falls back to day.

get.description: Organization-wide activity trail for the caller's current organization. The organization is always taken from the session, never from a parameter. Auth: scope READ_AUDIT_LOGS, org permission view_analytics.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Page size. Defaults to 50; must be between 10 and 200 or a 400 is returned.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque cursor from pagination.next_cursor. Invalid cursors return 400.

parameters.name: actor_id
parameters.description: Filter to a single acting member.

parameters.name: entity_type
parameters.description: Filter by entity type (for example campaign, contact, email_account, api_key, webhook).

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: Filter by action (for example create, update, delete, send, revoke).

parameters.name: date
parameters.description: Single-day filter (YYYY-MM-DD), expanded to that whole UTC day.

parameters.name: start_date
parameters.description: Range start. RFC 3339 or YYYY-MM-DD. Overrides date.

parameters.name: end_date
parameters.description: Range end. RFC 3339 or YYYY-MM-DD. Overrides date.

get.description: Static metadata for every provider Warmbly supports, annotated with whether each OAuth provider has server-side credentials wired (`configured`).
This org's connection rows. Secrets are never serialized. Returns a bare `connections` array, not the cursor-paginated envelope.

post.description: Creates a credential-based connection for `api_key` / `webhook` providers (e.g. Close, Discord). OAuth providers are rejected with a hint to start the authorize flow instead. Inbound providers (Calendly, Cal.com) include `inbound_webhook_url` once. Requires the `manage_settings` org permission for JWT callers.

400.description: Bad request (e.g. OAuth provider, invalid provider).

get.description: One connection plus its event subscriptions and up to 20 recent sync runs.

delete.description: Removes a connection row. Requires `manage_settings` for JWT callers.

patch.description: Saves a connection's onboarding/capability snapshot and its sync direction. Requires `manage_settings` for JWT callers.

get.description: The event-to-action routes configured on a connection.

post.description: Routes a Warmbly event to a provider action on this connection. Requires `manage_settings` for JWT callers.

delete.description: Removes one event subscription. Requires `manage_settings` for JWT callers.

get.description: The Warmbly-field to provider-field maps configured for a connection.

put.description: Swaps the connection-default field map for an object wholesale. A full replace is naturally idempotent so no `Idempotency-Key` is required. Requires `manage_settings` for JWT callers.

200.description: Full mapping set after the replace.

get.description: Up to 50 recent observability records for a connection (connect, token refresh, event dispatch, manual push).
Returns (generating on first call) the HMAC signing secret for an automation connection so you can verify Warmbly's outbound webhook signatures. Requires `manage_settings` for JWT callers.

post.description: Fires a synthetic event through the connection's notify/webhook automations so you can confirm the channel is wired. Requires `manage_settings` for JWT callers.

Synchronously upserts the given org contacts into a connected CRM (HubSpot, Pipedrive, Salesforce, Close). Retries are naturally safe (every upsert is keyed by email), so no `Idempotency-Key` is required. Requires the `use_integrations` org permission for JWT callers. A connection whose token can no longer be refreshed returns 409.

404.description: Connection or matching contacts not found.

409.description: Connection needs to be reconnected (token not refreshable).

get.description: Up to 50 recent booked meetings, surfaced on the integrations page. For the full Meetings list with filters and pagination, use `GET /meetings`.
This org's automation flows (the visual flow builder). Returns a bare `automations` array.

post.description: Creates a new automation flow: a trigger event plus a graph of condition and action nodes. Action nodes may reference provider actions (e.g. `slack.notify`, `hubspot.upsert_contact`) or Warmbly-native actions (e.g. `warmbly.add_tag`, `warmbly.label_email`) that need no external connection.

get.description: One automation with its full graph.

patch.description: Replaces an automation's name, enabled state, trigger, filter, and graph. The body shape matches the create payload.

delete.description: Removes an automation. Returns 409 when the automation is still referenced by campaign steps.

post.description: Runs the automation against sample (or provided) data without side effects and returns the walked trace plus per-action previews.

200.description: Dry-run trace plus resolved event data.

get.description: Recent run history for an automation (per fired event or manual launch), with per-node outcomes.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Max runs to return. Defaults to 50.

get.description: Returns the current organization's teams, each hydrated with its members. Requires a selected organization.

400.description: No organization selected or invalid request.

403.description: Key lacks the READ_CRM scope or caller lacks view_contacts.

post.description: Creates a team (members start empty). Requires a selected organization.

400.description: Validation error or no organization selected.

403.description: Key lacks the WRITE_CRM scope or caller lacks manage_team.

get.description: Returns a single team with its members.

400.description: Invalid id or no organization selected.

403.description: Key lacks the READ_CRM scope or caller lacks view_contacts.

patch.description: Partial-updates a team's name or color. Omitted fields are left untouched.

400.description: Validation error, invalid id, or no organization selected.

403.description: Key lacks the WRITE_CRM scope or caller lacks manage_team.

delete.description: Deletes a team.

400.description: Invalid id or no organization selected.

403.description: Key lacks the WRITE_CRM scope or caller lacks manage_team.

post.description: Adds an existing organization member to the team and returns the updated team.

200.description: The updated team, including the new member.

400.description: Validation error, invalid id, or no organization selected.

403.description: Key lacks the WRITE_CRM scope or caller lacks manage_team.

404.description: Team not found, or the user is not an organization member.

delete.description: Removes a member from the team.

400.description: Invalid id or no organization selected.

403.description: Key lacks the WRITE_CRM scope or caller lacks manage_team.

get.description: Returns the available public subscription plans. Open to any authenticated caller (JWT or API key); auth exists only to deter scraping.
Returns the supported timezone identifiers (for campaign schedule windows and the like). Open to any authenticated caller (JWT or API key).

200.description: The supported timezones, sorted by UTC offset.

get.description: Returns the organization's advanced outreach settings (bounce pipeline, task reliability, A/B testing, reply-intent, send-time optimization, preflight, dashboard).

200.description: The advanced outreach settings object (not envelope-wrapped).

403.description: Forbidden (missing WRITE_CAMPAIGNS scope or manage_settings permission)

patch.description: Replaces the organization's advanced outreach settings with the supplied object (upserted, not deep-merged). Returns no body.

post.description: Posts a single deliverability signal (bounce, complaint, unsubscribe, open, click, reply) into the platform. API-key callable so downstream pipelines can report events. Supply idempotency_key to make retries safe. Requires WRITE_CAMPAIGNS scope and send_campaigns permission.

202.description: Event accepted and queued for processing (no body).

400.description: Invalid event payload (e.g. missing event_type or recipient_email)

get.description: Lists tasks that exhausted their retry budget and landed in the dead-letter queue. Not cursor-paginated: returns up to `limit` rows in one response. Requires SEND_CAMPAIGNS scope and send_campaigns permission.

post.description: Re-dispatches a dead-lettered task. Because a replay can transmit real mail this requires SEND_CAMPAIGNS scope and send_campaigns permission.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The dead-letter record ID (the `id` field, not `task_id`).

get.description: Returns every warmup routing rule for the organization, ordered by priority ascending. Not cursor-paginated. Requires WARMUP_ROUTING scope and manage_settings permission.

200.description: Routing rules under a `rules` array (never null).

post.description: Creates a routing rule for the organization. Both sender and recipient sides are matched; a rule applies only when both match. Match values are lowercased and trimmed on write. Requires WARMUP_ROUTING scope and manage_settings permission.

400.description: Invalid payload (e.g. missing name, bad match type, missing required match value, negative weight)

patch.description: Replaces a rule by ID. The body is the same full payload as create (all fields applied, not deep-merged). Requires WARMUP_ROUTING scope and manage_settings permission.

delete.description: Removes a routing rule by ID. Requires WARMUP_ROUTING scope and manage_settings permission.

get.description: Lists the organization's reply templates, ordered by position. Optional `q` filter matches name and subject (case-insensitive). Not cursor-paginated. Requires READ_TEMPLATES scope and view_campaigns permission.

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: Optional case-insensitive search over name and subject.

post.description: Creates a reply template owned by the calling user, appended to the end of the org's list. Requires WRITE_TEMPLATES scope and manage_campaigns permission.

400.description: Invalid payload (e.g. missing name or name over 255 chars)

patch.description: Repositions templates to match the supplied ID order (1-indexed). IDs omitted from the list are left untouched. Returns the full reordered list. Requires WRITE_TEMPLATES scope and manage_campaigns permission.

post.description: Returns an advisory deliverability content score (0 to 100, higher is safer) for a subject and body, plus the issues found. Advisory only and never blocks sending. Scores content in the request body, not a stored template. Requires READ_TEMPLATES scope and view_campaigns permission.

get.description: Retrieves a single reply template by ID. Requires READ_TEMPLATES scope and view_campaigns permission.

patch.description: Updates a reply template. All fields optional; omitted fields are left unchanged. Requires WRITE_TEMPLATES scope and manage_campaigns permission.

delete.description: Deletes a reply template by ID. Requires WRITE_TEMPLATES scope and manage_campaigns permission.

post.description: Clones a template, appending " (copy)" to the name and placing the clone at the end of the org's list. Requires WRITE_TEMPLATES scope and manage_campaigns permission.

Expands {{.Key}} placeholders in the template's subject and body using a caller-supplied variable map. The body is optional; an empty map renders all placeholders empty. Requires READ_TEMPLATES scope and view_campaigns permission.

bearerAuth.description: A Warmbly API key (wmbly_...) sent as a Bearer token.

IdempotencyKey.name: Idempotency-Key
IdempotencyKey.description: Optional client-generated key (1-255 chars). A retried request with the same key, method, path, and body replays the original response instead of acting twice.

Error.description: Standard error envelope returned for every 4xx/5xx response.

error.description: Human-readable message. Do not branch on this.

message.description: Alias of error in some responses.

code.description: Stable machine-readable code, e.g. bad_request, unauthorized, forbidden, not_found, conflict, rate_limit_exceeded, internal_error.

request_id.description: Correlates the response with server logs. Also returned as the X-Request-Id header.

Pagination.description: Keyset pagination metadata. next_cursor is an opaque token (not a record id).

total.description: Total matching rows when cheaply known, else null.

next_cursor.description: Opaque cursor for the next page, or null on the last page.

AuthCredentials.description: Email/password credentials with a Cloudflare Turnstile token. Used for login start and registration start.

ConfirmRequest.description: Confirms an in-flight login or registration: the opaque session handle plus the emailed one-time code.

session.description: Opaque session handle from the matching start call.

code.description: One-time confirmation code emailed to the user.

AuthSession.description: Opaque session handle returned by login/register start; pass it to the matching confirm call.

TokenPair.description: An access/refresh token pair with absolute expiry timestamps.

access_token.description: Bearer access token for authenticated session requests.

refresh_token.description: Refresh token used to obtain a new pair.

LoginResult.description: Result of /auth/login/confirm: either a full token pair (the TokenPair fields are present) or a 2FA challenge (two_fa_required=true with a pending_token). The two cases are mutually exclusive.

access_token.description: Present only when 2FA is not required.

two_fa_required.description: True when a 2FA challenge must be completed via /auth/2fa/verify.

pending_token.description: Single-use pending token to pass to /auth/2fa/verify. Present only when two_fa_required is true.

expires_in.description: Seconds until the pending token expires. Present only when two_fa_required is true.

email.description: Email of the account to reset.

UpdateProfileRequest.description: Editable profile fields for the authenticated user.

pending_token.description: Single-use pending token from the login result.

code.description: A current TOTP code or a recovery code.

TwoFACodeRequest.description: A single TOTP or recovery code. Used to confirm enrollment or disable 2FA.

code.description: A current TOTP code or a recovery code.

TwoFAStatus.description: Whether 2FA is enabled for the user.

TwoFAEnrollStart.description: The TOTP secret and otpauth provisioning URI, returned once at enrollment start.

otpauth_uri.description: otpauth://totp/... provisioning URI for authenticator apps.

TwoFARecoveryCodes.description: One-time recovery codes, returned once when 2FA is enabled.

PasskeyLoginChallenge.description: WebAuthn assertion options plus the opaque login session handle for /auth/passkey/login/finish. The publicKey options follow the WebAuthn PublicKeyCredentialRequestOptions shape.

session.description: Opaque login session handle from begin.

credential.description: The WebAuthn assertion (PublicKeyCredential JSON) from navigator.credentials.get.

PasskeyRenameRequest.description: Request body for renaming a passkey.

name.description: New display name for the passkey.

transports.description: Supported authenticator transports (e.g. internal, usb, hybrid).

backup_state.description: Whether the credential is backed up / multi-device.

PasskeyCredentialList.description: A plain array of registered passkeys (not a paginated wrapper).

Session.description: A customer-facing view of an authenticated session.

current.description: True if this is the session making the request.

auth_provider.description: How this session authenticated: email, google, apple, or webauthn.

SessionList.description: A plain array of active sessions (not a paginated wrapper); the current session is floated to the top.

UserLabelGroup.description: A per-user label group (folder, tag, or category).

User.description: The authenticated user profile returned by /auth/me.

roles.description: Organization role ids assigned to the user.

is_admin.description: True if the user has any platform-admin permission.

campaign_limit.description: Daily cold-campaign cap for this mailbox.

warmup.description: Warmup anchor timestamp; null when warmup has never been enabled.

warmup_paused_at.description: Non-null when warmup is enabled but paused.

MailboxUpdate.description: Mailbox settings patch. All fields optional; only present fields are applied.

signature_code.description: Treat the HTML signature as raw code.

warmup_reply_rate.description: Percentage of warmup threads to reply to.

tags.description: Tag ids assigned to the mailbox.

MailboxTrackingDomain.description: The resolved custom tracking-domain state for a mailbox.

tracking_domain_verified_at.description: Null until the CNAME resolves to the tracking host.

MailboxAuthCheck.description: On-demand SPF/DKIM/DMARC check for the mailbox's sending domain.

spf_record.description: Omitted when SPF is not found.

dkim_selectors.description: Omitted when DKIM is not found.

dmarc_policy.description: Omitted when DMARC is not found.

MailboxVerifyRequest.description: Address to verify. Required if the email query param is not set.

MailboxWarmupBanStatus.description: Whether a mailbox is blocked from the shared warmup pool.

reason.description: The owner's explanation for the appeal.

MailboxSendRequest.description: A one-off send from a specific mailbox.

in_reply_to.description: Message ids this email replies to.

thread_id.description: Thread id to attach the message to.

send_mode.description: instant (default), smart (next per-mailbox scheduler gap), or scheduled (use scheduled_at).

scheduled_at.description: Required when send_mode is scheduled. Must be in the future.

ScheduleWindows.description: Per-day sending schedule. 7-element array indexed by time.Weekday (Sunday = 0); each day is a list of {start, end} minute intervals. When non-empty it supersedes days/start_time/end_time.

days.description: Legacy weekday bitmask (0-127), superseded by schedule_windows.

email_tags.description: Mailbox tag ids resolving the sender pool (tags strategy).

CampaignCreate.description: Only name is required; every other field is optional and applied only when sent.

days.description: Legacy weekday bitmask (0-127), superseded by schedule_windows.

email_tag_ids.description: Mailbox tag ids that resolve the sender pool (tags strategy).

variants.description: A/B variants for the first step.

CampaignUpdate.description: Every field optional; any field sent is applied, omitted fields unchanged.

CampaignStep.description: A sequence step (email or action/condition node).

body_sync.description: Keep plain and HTML bodies in sync.

body_code.description: Treat the body as raw code.

wait_after.description: Minutes to wait after this step before the next.

CampaignStepCreate.description: Initial step shape used inside campaign create (sequences[]).

CampaignStepUpdate.description: Patch a step. All fields optional.

wait_after.description: Minutes to wait after this step (spacing model; no standalone wait node for email steps).

conditions.description: Branching tree ({branches: [...]}). Send {} or empty branches to clear branching.

action.description: Typed config for non-email nodes; type is the switch (wait, add_tag, remove_tag, unsubscribe, notify, create_task, create_deal, move_deal_stage, run_automation, end).

step_id.description: Step the variant is scoped to; null is campaign-level.
Step to scope the variant to; omit for campaign-level.

CampaignABVariantUpdate.description: Patch a variant. All fields optional.

AdvancedOutreachSettings.description: Advanced outreach overrides for a campaign.

step_id.description: Step to render and send; defaults to the first step.

contact.description: Override fields on the built-in sample contact.

errors.description: Template parse errors that would block sending. Omitted when empty.

unresolved.description: Literal {{...}} tokens left after render. Omitted when empty.

MiniCategory.description: Denormalised category chip attached to a contact.

MiniCampaign.description: Denormalised campaign reference attached to a contact.

ContactCampaignProgress.description: A contact's aggregate processing state inside one campaign. Present on search results only when filtering by exactly one campaign.

current_step.description: Label of the step the contact is on now. Empty when nothing sent yet.

esp_provider.description: Recipient ESP derived from the domain: '' | gmail | outlook | other.

campaign_lead.description: Present only when search filters by exactly one campaign.

ContactEngagement.description: Aggregate email engagement summary for a single contact.

ContactSuppression.description: Suppression state for the contact's email. Null when not suppressed.

ContactDetail.description: The hydrated contact 360 payload: the contact plus engagement and suppression.

ContactSearchRequest.description: Faceted contact search filters. All fields optional.

query.description: Text search across name, email, company.

campaign_ids.description: Contact must be in ALL of these campaigns.

category_ids.description: Contact must have ALL of these categories.

campaigns.description: Campaign IDs to add the contact to.

ContactUpdate.description: Partial update for a single contact. Only present fields change.

campaigns.description: Set the full campaign membership (omit to leave as-is).

categories.description: Set the full category list (omit to leave as-is).

add_categories.description: Diff-style add (ignored when categories is set).

remove_categories.description: Diff-style remove (ignored when categories is set).

ContactBulkUpdateRequest.description: One set of edits applied across many contacts.

subscribe.description: Set subscription status for all listed contacts.

ContactBulkFieldOp.description: A custom-field operation applied during a bulk update.

contact_ids.description: Contact IDs when scope is selected.

filters.description: A search filter body when scope is filtered.

fields.description: Column identifiers in display order (built-ins like email, first_name, or custom:<key>). Empty uses defaults.

filename.description: Filename without extension. Sanitized server-side; empty falls back to contacts-<YYYY-MM-DD>.

ContactImportColumnMapping.description: Maps a source column index to a target contact field.

target.description: Target field, or a custom:<key> string for a custom field.

custom_key.description: Custom-field key when target is custom:<key>.

ContactImportPreview.description: Detected columns and a sample for building a column mapping.

ContactImportResult.description: Per-row results of an import commit.

ContactLookupResult.description: Resolved contact for a sender address; contact is null when nothing matches.

ContactSentEmail.description: One email sent (or attempted) to a contact.

ContactSentEmailList.description: A page of emails sent to a contact.

ContactTimelineEvent.description: One entry in a contact's merged activity feed. Fields not relevant to the event type are omitted.

scheduled_for.description: When the call is set for (meeting events).

ContactTimelineResult.description: A page of timeline events. Paginate via has_more and the `before` query param; this list does not use a cursor envelope.

ContactNote.description: A CRM note attached to a contact.

ContactNoteCreate.description: Body to create a contact note.

ContactNoteUpdate.description: Body to edit a contact note.

Deal.description: A CRM deal. Nullable attribution fields are omitted when unset.

UniboxLabel.description: A conversation label (one of the caller's categories).

UniboxThread.description: One inbox row, summarising the newest message of a thread plus thread-level rollups.

id.description: UUID of the newest message in the thread.

email_id.description: UUID of the mailbox the message landed in.

seen.description: Whether the newest message is read.

message_count.description: Number of messages in the thread.

has_unread.description: Whether the thread has any unread message.

UniboxMessage.description: A full message row inside a thread (envelope plus body).

email_id.description: UUID of the mailbox the message landed in.

UniboxEmail.description: A single message fetched by id. Note the field names differ from the thread message shape (e.g. `from`/`to`/`date`).

UniboxOverviewBucket.description: A tag or conversation-label breakdown bucket.

category_ids.description: Full desired set of category UUIDs. An empty array clears all labels.

seen.description: `true` marks as read, `false` marks as unread.

scheduled_at.description: Required when `send_mode` is `scheduled`; must be in the future.
When the send is scheduled to fire.

snoozed_until.description: RFC 3339 timestamp to un-hide the thread.

user_id.description: Snoozes are attached to the calling user.

UniboxScheduledSend.description: A preview of a queued, not-yet-sent outbound message.

Pipeline.description: A sales pipeline with its ordered stages.

deal_count.description: Count of deals in this stage (populated by the pipeline list/get queries).

stages.description: Stages to create with the pipeline, in order.

source_mailbox_id.description: Sending mailbox that produced the originating reply.

UpdateDeal.description: All fields optional. Moving stage_id records a stage-change activity; setting status to won/lost stamps the close timestamp.

stage_id.description: Move the deal to this stage.

SearchDeals.description: Faceted deal filter body shared by deal search and deal summary. All facets optional; an empty body matches every deal.

assigned_to.description: Owner is any of these user IDs.

min_value.description: Value greater than or equal to.

max_value.description: Value less than or equal to.

close_after.description: Expected close date on or after.

close_before.description: Expected close date on or before.

DealsSearchResult.description: Offset-paginated deal search result with an exact total.

DealsSummary.description: Aggregate counts and value sums over a deal search filter body. mixed_currency true means top-level value sums should be treated as approximate.

CRMTaskType.description: A user-managed CRM task type (the kind of work a task represents).

CRMTask.description: A CRM task (follow-up work attached to contacts and deals).

type.description: Task type name (matches a configured task type).

UpdateCRMTask.description: All fields optional. Setting status to completed stamps the completion timestamp.

SearchTasks.description: Faceted task filter body shared by task search and task summary. All facets optional; an empty body matches every task.

types.description: Task type name is any of these.

assigned_to.description: Assignee user ID is any of these.

team_ids.description: Task team is any of these, or the assignee belongs to one.

overdue.description: Only tasks past due and not completed or cancelled.

TasksSearchResult.description: Offset-paginated task search result with an exact total.

TasksSummary.description: Aggregate task counts over a task search filter body.

APIKey.description: An API key without its plaintext secret.

user_id.description: The member who created the key.

description.description: Free-form note about the key's purpose.

key_prefix.description: Short display prefix of the secret (e.g. wmbly_3f).

key_suffix.description: Short display suffix of the secret.

permissions.description: uint64 permission bitmask. Combine bits with bitwise OR; the key may perform a request only when its mask contains every required bit. See GET /api-keys/permissions for bit names and values.

allowed_ips.description: If non-empty, the key is usable only from these source IPs.

allowed_email_accounts.description: If non-empty, mailbox-scoped routes accept only these email account ids.

rate_limit_per_minute.description: Per-key sliding-window request cap. 0 means the default (60 r/m).

expires_at.description: When the key stops working. Null for a non-expiring key.

APIKeyWithSecret.description: An API key plus the one-time plaintext secret, returned only on creation.

secret.description: The full plaintext key. Returned only once, at creation; it cannot be recovered later.

description.description: Free-form note about the key's purpose.

permissions.description: uint64 permission bitmask. Must contain only defined bits; unknown bits are rejected.

allowed_ips.description: If set, the key is usable only from these source IPs. Omit or leave empty to allow any IP.

allowed_email_accounts.description: If set, mailbox-scoped routes accept only these email account ids.

rate_limit_per_minute.description: Per-key request cap. Omit or send 0 to use the default (60 r/m).

expires_at.description: When the key should stop working (RFC3339). Omit for a non-expiring key.

UpdateAPIKey.description: Every field is optional; only the fields you send are changed.

rate_limit_per_minute.description: New per-key rate cap. 0 means use the default.

APIKeyUsageSummary.description: Org-level usage strip. The 24h fields cover the last 24 hours.

APIKeyUsageBucket.description: One point on a time-bucketed request graph.

APIKeyEndpointStat.description: One row in the per-endpoint breakdown.

api_key_id.description: The key id, or the all-zero UUID for the org-wide aggregate.

APIKeyUsageLog.description: One recorded request made with the key.

WebhookEventType.description: Canonical event name carried in the subscription filter and the delivery payload. An endpoint with an empty `event_types` filter receives all of these.

WebhookEndpoint.description: A customer's subscription to events. Returned without the signing secret on all reads and updates.

url.description: HTTPS URL that receives POST callbacks.

description.description: Free-text label for your own reference.

event_types.description: Subscribed event names. An empty array means all events.

last_success_at.description: Time of the last successful delivery, or null.

last_failure_at.description: Time of the last failed delivery, or null.

last_failure_reason.description: Reason for the last failure, or null.

consecutive_failures.description: Current run of consecutive delivery failures.

secret.description: The `whsec_`-prefixed HMAC signing secret. Returned only at create and rotate time.

WebhookEndpointRequest.description: Create/update body. The same body shape is used for both; on update all fields are replaced with the values sent (send the complete desired state). The secret is server-generated and cannot be set here.

url.description: HTTPS URL that will receive POST callbacks. Must be publicly routable.

description.description: Free-text label for your own reference.

event_types.description: Event names to subscribe to. Each must be a known type. An empty or omitted array subscribes to all events. On update this overwrites the existing filter (not merged).

enabled.description: Whether the endpoint is active. Defaults to true.

WebhookEndpointList.description: List response. Not a `data` + `pagination` cursor envelope: it returns the configured endpoints plus the full event vocabulary.

event_types.description: Every event Warmbly can emit, for building a picker.

secret.description: The new `whsec_`-prefixed HMAC signing secret. Returned only once.

WebhookDeliveryStatus.description: Lifecycle state of a delivery attempt. `abandoned` means retries were exhausted.

WebhookDelivery.description: One delivery-history record. The row updates in place across retries; `attempt_count` and `status` reflect the latest state.

event_id.description: Stable across retries. Matches the `X-Warmbly-Event-Id` header and the payload `id`.

attempt_count.description: Number of delivery attempts made so far.

max_attempts.description: Maximum attempts before the delivery is abandoned (default 8).

next_attempt_at.description: Scheduled time of the next retry.

response_status.description: HTTP status returned by the subscriber on the last attempt, or null.

response_body_excerpt.description: First 1024 bytes of the subscriber's response, or null.

error_reason.description: Connection or timeout error from the last attempt, or null.

WebhookDeliveryList.description: Delivery list response, newest first. Not a `data` + `pagination` cursor envelope; bounded by the `limit` query param.

WebhookPayload.description: The JSON body POSTed to a subscriber endpoint. Signed with HMAC-SHA256: the `X-Warmbly-Signature` header is `t=<unix>,v1=<hex>`, where `v1 = hex(hmac_sha256(secret, "<t>." + rawBody))`. Other headers: `X-Warmbly-Event` (event type), `X-Warmbly-Event-Id` (the event id for dedupe), `User-Agent: Warmbly-Webhooks/1.0`.

id.description: Unique event id, stable across retries. Matches `X-Warmbly-Event-Id`.

created_at.description: RFC 3339 UTC timestamp of when the event was dispatched.

type.description: Activity type, for example replied, opened, clicked.

DeliverabilityDashboard.description: Deliverability posture over a window. spam_placement_rate and inbox_placement_rate are omitted when there are no seed samples.

spam_placement_rate.description: Omitted when there are no seed samples in the window.

inbox_placement_rate.description: Omitted when there are no seed samples in the window.

WarmupAnalytics.description: Warmup send and reply statistics over a date range. email_account_id is the zero UUID when no email_id filter is supplied.

CampaignAnalytics.description: A single campaign's performance summary plus per-step stats.

CampaignAnalyticsSummary.description: machine_opens is the subset of unique_opens from automated fetchers; human opens are unique_opens minus machine_opens.

CampaignDailyStats.description: Per-day campaign series under a data envelope.

CampaignHourlyStats.description: Per-hour campaign series under a data envelope with the resolved date echoed back.

CampaignComparison.description: Side-by-side performance for up to 10 campaigns over a date range.

AccountStatus.description: Summary health and usage status of an email account.

allOf.description: warmup_status is present only when warmup has ever been enabled; warmup_health is present only when the mailbox is in a warmup pool.

AccountStatusList.description: All of the caller's account statuses under a data envelope (no cursor).

UsageOverview.description: Account, campaign, contact, and API usage counters for the caller.

AuditLog.description: One organization audit-trail entry. actor is null when the acting user has since been deleted; entity_id, changes, and metadata are omitted when empty.

changes.description: Field-level change set; secret values are never recorded.

AuditLogList.description: A page of audit logs with an opaque cursor.

IntegrationCatalogEntry.description: Static metadata for one provider the dashboard renders even when no connection exists.

scopes.description: OAuth scopes requested at authorize time.

events.description: Warmbly events this provider can react to.

action_types.description: Provider action identifiers with a real backend handler.

supports_push.description: Whether this provider can be the target of the synchronous push-contacts action.

capability.description: Configurable-action descriptor the dashboard renders onboarding + field-mapping UI from.

configured.description: Whether the server has OAuth client credentials wired for this provider.

IntegrationConnection.description: One org's link to one provider. Secrets are never serialized.

inbound_webhook_url.description: Returned only at create time for inbound providers (Calendly, Cal.com).

IntegrationConnectionDetail.description: A connection plus its event subscriptions and recent sync runs (the detail drawer payload).

provider.description: A valid api_key/webhook provider id (e.g. close, discord). OAuth providers are rejected.

label.description: Friendly name shown on the connection card.

config.description: Provider-specific config (e.g. the pasted API key or webhook URL).

config_capabilities.description: Per-connection capability snapshot (picker selections, enabled use-cases).

IntegrationEventSubscription.description: Routes a Warmbly event to a provider (or native) action on a connection.

event_type.description: The Warmbly event to react to (e.g. email.replied).

action.description: Provider action id (e.g. slack.notify, hubspot.upsert_contact) or native action (e.g. warmbly.add_tag, warmbly.label_email).

config.description: Action config (e.g. a Slack channel or message template).

use_case.description: Discriminator describing what this automation is for (e.g. crm_sync, notify, custom).

automation_id.description: Set when this subscription is one step of an Automation flow.

event_type.description: The Warmbly event to react to (e.g. email.replied).

config.description: Action config (e.g. a Slack channel or message template).

IntegrationFieldMapping.description: One Warmbly-field to provider-field mapping row.

transform.description: One of '' (none), none, static, uppercase, lowercase, trim.

object.description: The provider object the mappings apply to (e.g. contact).

mappings.description: The full set of mappings to store (replaces any existing).

external_field.description: Destination field on the provider. Required for every mapping.

warmbly_field.description: Source Warmbly field. Required unless transform is 'static'.

transform.description: One of '' (none), none, static, uppercase, lowercase, trim.

IntegrationSyncRun.description: One observability record of work done against a connection.

signing_secret.description: HMAC signing secret used to verify outbound webhook signatures.

contact_ids.description: Contact ids to push. Deduplicated server-side. At least 1, at most 500.

MeetingBooking.description: One booked meeting from a connected scheduling provider (Calendly, Cal.com) or a manually logged meeting.

Automation.description: A branching flow: when the trigger event fires, the executor walks the graph, evaluating condition nodes and running the action nodes on matched paths.

trigger_event.description: The event that fires the flow (e.g. email.replied).

filter.description: Optional automation-wide gate (e.g. intents / min_confidence) applied to every action.

AutomationWrite.description: Create/update payload from the flow builder.

enabled.description: Whether the automation runs on matching events.

trigger_event.description: The event that fires the flow (e.g. email.replied).

filter.description: Optional automation-wide gate applied to every action.

AutomationGraph.description: The editable flow: nodes plus the edges connecting them.

id.description: Node id. The single trigger node uses id 'trigger'.

type.description: Exactly one trigger node; condition nodes have true/false outgoing edges; action nodes run a handler.

action.description: Action node only: provider action (e.g. slack.notify) or native action (e.g. warmbly.add_tag, warmbly.label_email, warmbly.run_automation).

connection_id.description: Action node only: the integration connection the action runs against. Omitted for native (warmbly.*) actions.

when.description: '' for plain edges; 'true' / 'false' for the two outgoing edges of a condition node.

AutomationCondition.description: An IF test evaluated against the trigger event's data.

key.description: For 'field' conditions, the event-data key to test.

expression.description: For 'expression' conditions, a Go-template predicate evaluated against the event data.

AutomationDryRunRequest.description: Optional. When omitted, the server builds a sample event from the trigger.

data.description: Sample event payload to evaluate the flow against.
The resolved event data the flow was evaluated against.

AutomationRun.description: One execution of an automation graph (per fired event or manual launch).

AutomationNodeResult.description: One node's outcome in a run (or a dry-run trace).

preview.description: Dry-run only: what the action would send.

Team.description: A named, color-tagged grouping of an organization's members.

color.description: Hex color for the chip (defaults to #94a3b8).

members.description: The members that belong to this team. Always an array (never null).

TeamMember.description: One membership row of a team, with the user's email and name joined for display.

TeamCollection.description: A bare collection of teams (not cursor-paginated).

TeamCreate.description: Request body for creating a team.

TeamUpdate.description: Partial-update body for a team. Omitted fields are left untouched.

TeamAddMember.description: Request body for adding an existing organization member to a team.

user_id.description: The member's user id (must already belong to the organization).

monthly_credits.description: AI writing-assistant monthly credit grant for this plan.

PlanList.description: The list of public subscription plans.

TimezoneOption.description: A supported timezone identifier and its display label.

recipient_email.description: The recipient address the event is about.

campaign_id.description: Campaign the event is attributed to.

task_id.description: Send task the event is attributed to.

contact_id.description: Contact the event is attributed to.

metadata.description: Free-form JSON attached to the event.

TaskDeadLetter.description: A task that exhausted its retry budget and landed in the dead-letter queue.

id.description: Dead-letter record ID (use this to replay).

TaskDeadLetterList.description: List of dead-letter records. Not cursor-paginated.

WarmupRoutingRule.description: A customer-defined preference applied during premium-pool partner selection.

sender_match_value.description: Lowercased/trimmed; empty when sender_match_type is any.

recipient_match_value.description: Lowercased/trimmed; empty when recipient_match_type is any.

WarmupRoutingRuleInput.description: Create/update payload for a warmup routing rule. A match value is required unless its match type is `any`.

priority.description: Evaluation order, ascending. Lower runs first.

sender_match_value.description: Required unless sender_match_type is any. Domain (acme.com), TLD (com), or provider bucket (google, microsoft, yahoo, apple, proton, zoho, custom).

recipient_match_value.description: Required unless recipient_match_type is any. Same value forms as the sender side.

WarmupRoutingRuleList.description: Warmup routing rules under a `rules` key (always an array, never null). Not cursor-paginated.

user_id.description: Owner (the user that created the template).

ReplyTemplateList.description: Reply templates under a `data` key. Not cursor-paginated.

UpdateReplyTemplate.description: All fields optional; omitted fields are left unchanged.

ids.description: Template IDs in their new order (1-indexed). IDs omitted are left untouched.

ScoreTemplateRequest.description: Content to score. body_plain is preferred over body_html when present.
